svg.text: Joining a Network — Sovereign “Model B” Federation
One signing root per principal, never cross-issued — and the one-command, plug-and-play join
Design ref: ADR-0013 · docs/sop-network-join.md · cortex network join · 2026-06-27
2 · Where signing happens — ONE root per principal
one seed you hold; cortex derives the rest
one root · distinct credentials, all derived — you never juggle them by hand
= your NSC operator — the one seed you hold
the account derives two distinct credentials
pinned as stack.nkey_pub · trusted via signed_by
Federation leaf attaches with the network’s leaf shared secret (PSK) — the one credential NOT derived from your root.
one seed you hold; cortex derives the rest. Distinct credentials — you never juggle them by hand.
The registry signs nothing. The hub admin mints NO creds for you.
The only thing handed over is a leaf shared secret — a transport PSK (like a Wi-Fi password), NOT an identity credential.
mostly self-serve — with ONE trust gate where someone else must say yes
STEPS 1–3 · self-sovereign — you act alone, no one’s permission needed
You mint your own Ed25519 key —
you sign, you own the seed.
the only place someone else must say yes
The admin (or admin-agent) approves your roster
membership → hands you the leaf shared secret.
① network membership — admin curates the roster
② per-principal accept-policy — each peer still
chooses whom to accept · network:<id> | principals:[…]
trust is granted AND chosen — never minted.
your NSC operator = you own your keys (your device’s identity)
the registry = DNS — resolve names → pubkeys; pin ONE resolver
leaf shared secret = the PSK to attach to the medium (transport, not identity)
cortex network join <network> = the DHCP-style plug-in
one verb — the registry autoconfigures the layers
Hand the registry a network name — it hands back everything the layers need:
descriptor + roster + trust anchor. No out-of-band key wrangling.
*one signing root per principal · one registry to resolve them · one PSK to attach*
metafactory · cortex M7 collaboration surface · sovereign “Model B” federation · docs/diagrams/cortex-network-join.png
